corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: In this work, we train a general-purpose text embedding model E5 from weak supervision signals.

context_window.previous: For long-tail domains such as Trec-Covid [ 55 ] and retrieval tasks that involve long documents (Touche-2020) [ 4 ] or rely heavily on exact lexical match (Fever) [ 54 ], further research efforts are still necessary to improve current dense retrievers.

top_candidates.sentence: When averaged over all 15 datasets, E5-PT base outperforms the classic BM25 algorithm by 1.2 points.
Our E5 base model achieves an average nDCG@10 of 48.7, already surpassing existing methods with more parameters such as GTR large .
In Table , E5 models not only substantially outperform existing ones with similar sizes, but also match the results of much larger models.
It remains an open question whether state-of-the-art embeddings can be obtained in a purely self-supervised manner.
This evaluation setup is consistent with most state-of-the-art dense retrievers.
In this work, we mine and assemble CCPairs, a large high-quality text pair dataset from web sources which provide diverse training signals transferring well to a wide range of tasks.
Instead, we curate a text pair dataset CCPairs (Colossal Clean text Pairs) by harvesting heterogeneous semi-structured data sources.

Our dataset includes (post, comment) pairs from Reddit , (question, upvoted answer) pairs from Stackexchange , (entity name + section title, passage) pairs from English Wikipedia, (title, abstract) and citation pairs from Scientific papers , and (title, passage) pairs from Common Crawl web pages and various News sources.
For example, we remove Reddit comments that are either too long (> 4096 characters) or receive score less than 1, and remove passages from web pages with high perplexity .
BEIR Benchmark is a collection of 19 information retrieval datasets, ranging across ad-hoc web search, question answering, fact verification and duplicate question retrieval, etc.
The evaluation metrics are accuracy, v-measure, average precision, MAP, nDCG@10, and Spearman coefficients, respectively.
The evaluation metrics are accuracy, v-measure, average precision, MAP, nDCG@10, and Spearman coefficients, respectively.
To the best of our knowledge, this is the first reported result that an unsupervised model can beat BM25 on the BEIR benchmark.
The highest number for each dataset is in bold, and the second highest is underlined. †: we report the LaPraDor results without ensembling with BM25. * : reproduction with the released checkpoint.
The methods can easily obtain large-scale training data, while our approach requires more effort in dataset curation.

Since the difference between BERT-FT base and E5 base is that BERT-FT base only has fine-tuning stage, their performance gap demonstrates the usefulness of contrastive pre-training on our proposed CCPairs dataset.
For the BEIR benchmark, we choose 6 datasets with more stable results across different runs.
For the Quora duplicate retrieval task in the BEIR benchmark, we add prefix "query: " to all the questions.
However, the MS-MARCO data from the BEIR benchmark does not have titles, so the results are expected to be lower.
Table : In-domain results. "target pre-train" refers to intermediate pre-training on the target corpus before supervised fine-tuning.

MS-MARCO NQ MRR@10 R@1k R@20 R@100 ANCE 61] ✗ 33.0 95.9 81.9 87.5 RocketQAv2 [50] ✗ 38.8 98.1 83.7 89.0 SimLM [58] ✓ 41.1 98.7 85.2 89.7 E5 small ✗ 37.5 98.1 84.6 89.8 E5 base ✗ 38.5 98.5 86.1 90.7 E5 large ✗ 39.4 98.7 86.4 90.5 Here are some attempts that we eventually give up on: Adding BM25 hard negatives Similar to DPR , we add one BM25 hard negative for each positive pair during training.
When using 15M data, this strategy improves the overall results by ∼ 0.5 points on the BEIR benchmark.
However, running the BM25 algorithm over a 250M+ dataset is too time-consuming even with multi-node and multi-process parallelism.

Using RoBERTa instead of BERT for initialization Though RoBERTa shows consistent gains on many NLP tasks, we empirically find that RoBERTa performs worse than BERT initialization on most of the BEIR benchmark datasets.
